title: Information Technology (IT) Department
learning_objectives: Understand the role of the IT Department.
Learn how IT supports manufacturing and business operations.
Understand IT infrastructure, networking and cybersecurity basics.
Recognize how IT manages enterprise applications such as SAP.
Understand the importance of data security and system reliability.

introduction.title: What is the IT Department?
introduction.content: The Information Technology (IT) Department is responsible for managing the organization's technology infrastructure, business applications, communication systems and cybersecurity. It ensures that employees have secure, reliable and efficient access to the digital tools required for daily operations.

business_purpose.title: Why is the IT Department Important?
business_purpose.content: Modern manufacturing relies heavily on digital systems. IT enables business continuity by maintaining servers, networks, SAP systems, databases and user devices. It also protects organizational data from cyber threats while supporting innovation and digital transformation.

department_objectives: Ensure uninterrupted operation of enterprise systems.
it_service_workflow: Resolve the issue or escalate if required.

key_responsibilities.description: Maintain servers, networks, internet connectivity and communication systems.
Support SAP, Learning Management Systems and other business applications.
Protect systems against unauthorized access, malware and cyber attacks.
Provide hardware and software support to employees.
Create user accounts, assign permissions and manage system access.
Ensure business data is securely backed up and recoverable.

daily_activities: Install software updates and security patches.
Support SAP and other enterprise applications.

key_it_concepts.description: Connects computers, servers and industrial systems for communication.
Protects systems, networks and information from digital threats.
Provides scalable storage and computing resources over the internet.
Restores critical systems after unexpected failures.

coordination_with_departments.purpose: Creates user accounts and system access for new employees.
Supports financial systems and data security.
Maintains SCADA, PLC communication and production applications.
Supports computerized maintenance systems and equipment connectivity.
Supports quality management applications and reporting.
Ensures SAP system performance, availability and user administration.

business_example.title: IT Operations at JSL
business_example.content: A new engineer joins the Production Department. HR sends a request to IT, which creates the employee's email account, SAP login and network access. During production, IT monitors SAP servers and SCADA connectivity to ensure uninterrupted plant operations. If a system issue occurs, the IT team investigates and restores services quickly to minimize operational impact.

best_practices: Use strong passwords and multi-factor authentication where available.
Keep systems updated with security patches.
Back up important business data regularly.
Restrict access based on job responsibilities.
common_errors: Failure to back up critical data.
important_notes: Information security is the responsibility of every employee.
System downtime can directly affect manufacturing operations.
Regular backups help protect business continuity.
Cybersecurity awareness is as important as technical security controls.
